records.ety: [OE. flean, flan, AS. flen; akin to D. vlaen, Icel. flā, Sw. fl, Dan. flaae, cf. Lith. pleszti to tear, plyszti, v.i., to burst, tear; perh. akin to E. flag a flat stone, flaw.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Flayed (flād); p. pr. & vb. n. Flaying.]

defs: To skin; to strip off the skin or surface of; as, to flay an ox; to flay the green earth.
quotes: With her nails She 'll flay thy wolfish visage.
